page_title: Super visa for parents and grandparents Proof of financial support - Canada.ca

h2: Step 1: Calculate family size to find your host’s minimum income requirements
Step 2: Provide proof of your host’s income
h3: Minimum income your host needs based on the size of the family

sections.heading: On this page
bullets: Calculate family size to find your host’s minimum income requirements
Provide proof of your host’s income

sections.heading: Step 1: Calculate family size to find your host’s minimum income requirements
items: To determine your host’s minimum necessary income, include the following people in the family size count:
bullets: you and any other super visa applicants the host will be supporting (such as your spouse or common-law partner)
the host child or grandchild
the host’s spouse (which might include a separated spouse ) or common-law partner

dependent children of the host and of their spouse or common-law partner Children who meet the definition of a dependent child must be included in the count, regardless of custody and child support arrangements.
Children who meet the definition of a dependent child must be included in the count, regardless of custody and child support arrangements.

previously approved super visa applicants These are super visa holders who were in another letter of invitation (that is still applicable) signed by the host or the host’s spouse or common-law partner.
These are super visa holders who were in another letter of invitation (that is still applicable) signed by the host or the host’s spouse or common-law partner.
previously sponsored individuals These are individuals the host or co-signer has previously sponsored (or acted as a co-signer for) and for whom the duration of the undertaking is still in effect .
These are individuals the host or co-signer has previously sponsored (or acted as a co-signer for) and for whom the duration of the undertaking is still in effect .

sections.heading: Minimum income your host needs based on the size of the family
Step 2: Provide proof of your host’s income
items: You must provide proof that your host meets the minimum necessary income requirements with your application. Your host can prove this in 1 of 2 ways.

sections.heading: Option 1
items: Your host can show that their total income (including the co-signer’s income, if applicable) meets or exceeds the minimum required amount in either of the 2 tax years before the application is submitted.
Your host, or their co-signer, must include their notice of assessment from the Canada Revenue Agency (CRA).

sections.heading: Option 2
items: Your host can show that their total income (including the co-signer’s income, if applicable) in the year before the application is submitted was at least 75% of the minimum required amount.
You can add your own income to cover the remaining amount, if needed. The combined amount must meet or exceed the minimum necessary income requirements .
